payload.summary: All three amendments are technically sound. One fixes misleading invariant handling, one documents an intentional legacy exception, and one identifies a real race that can produce false plan_hash conflicts.

votes.rationale: `unique_terminal_replay_match` currently collapses a caller contract violation into the same `None` path used for an ordinary no-match, so downstream code reports a misleading "no matching task run" error. Even though the current internal call sites satisfy the contract, the runtime guard does not actually return an error as the issue requires; it only logs at `warn`. Propagating a distinct `AppError` is the robust fix, and at minimum this should be logged as an error-level programming fault rather than a recoverable warning.

The current `safe_plan_hash_backfill` behavior for `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation=None` is intentional and safety-driven, but the nearby Issue 3 wording broadly says omitted `plan_hash` values auto-populate from the snapshot, which is not true for these legacy rows. Adding explicit comments in the code and regression test to mark this as a deliberate exception improves maintainability and reduces the risk of future reviewers treating the preserved `None` as an accidental regression. This is low-risk and clarifies an otherwise easy-to-misread edge case.

This race exists in the current implementation. `record_task_run_start` stamps a new row with the live snapshot hash when `plan_hash` is omitted, and `update_task_run` later treats that stored value as authoritative, so a dispatch under plan v1 can be persisted as v2 if the snapshot advances before start is recorded. Finalization with the real v1 hash then fails with a false `conflicting plan_hash` error. A regression test for that sequence is warranted, and the storage layer should not infer `plan_hash` from the current snapshot unless it can prove that snapshot matches the dispatched attempt.
